title: Aureline restore-provenance and missing-dependency placeholder record
description: Boundary schema for the cross-surface restore-provenance, compatibility-restore downgrade, and missing-dependency placeholder contract. Captures source, created-at, producer build, source schema version, redaction class, resulting fidelity, restore level, missing-dependency classes, missing-dependency placeholder cards (preserving original pane role and recovery actions), schema-migration notes, preserved-prior-artifact refs, rollback and equivalence-map handles, intentional exclusions, and compare/export hooks. The closed five-class fidelity vocabulary (exact, compatible, layout_only, recovered_drafts, evidence_only) is the same one used by startup, support, and docs/help flows. See docs/state/restore_provenance_and_placeholder_contract.md.

restore_provenance_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for the restore-provenance and placeholder record. Bumped only on breaking payload changes.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the restore-provenance and placeholder record.

opaque_id.description: Opaque stable identifier safe for logs, manifests, support exports, and compare handles. Raw paths, raw URLs, credentials, source content, command lines, and live authority handles never appear here.

nullable_opaque_id.description: Opaque handle or null when the handle is intentionally absent.

monotonic_timestamp.description: Producer-local monotonic timestamp. Opaque to this schema.

redaction_aware_text.description: Redaction-aware free text (<= 1024 graphemes). Exporters apply the active redaction policy before crossing this boundary.

fixture_metadata.description: Optional worked-example prelude carried on fixtures.

producer_name.description: Producer name, e.g. 'aureline'. Never a raw hostname.

producer_version.description: Producer version string. Opaque at the schema boundary.

artifact_family.description: High-level artifact family the provenance record describes.

source_class.description: Concrete source-class label for the artifact the restore consumed.

source_event_class.description: User-visible source event family that caused the restore provenance record to be emitted.

redaction_class.description: Shared redaction-class set reused by support export and state portability surfaces.

fidelity_class.description: Closed five-class compatibility-restore downgrade vocabulary. Display copy may render Exact, Compatible, Layout only, Recovered drafts, and Evidence only.

restore_level.description: Restore-level vocabulary aligned with the fidelity class. The closed set is fixed; partial/best_effort labels are non-conforming.

source_artifact_ref.description: Opaque ref for the source artifact the restore consumed.

source_summary.description: What the restore was asked to reopen.

missing_dependency_class.description: Closed taxonomy of missing-dependency classes that drive a placeholder card.

preserved_pane_role.description: Original pane role the placeholder card stands in for. Re-export of the workspace-shell pane-tree role vocabulary minus 'placeholder' (a placeholder card never stands in for another placeholder).

preserved_surface_class.description: Original surface class the placeholder card stands in for. Re-export of the workspace-shell pane-tree class vocabulary minus 'placeholder_card'.

recovery_action.description: Closed recovery-action vocabulary on a placeholder card. Aligned with the placeholder-action set frozen on the window-topology snapshot.

restore_without_rerun_label.description: Closed label set for surfaces restored without rerunning commands or silently reacquiring authority.

runtime_survived.description: True only when the backing runtime survived and current authority was verified.

command_rerun_forbidden.description: Commands, tasks, cells, and sessions are not rerun by restore.

authority_reacquire_forbidden.description: Credentials, tickets, routes, remote sessions, and shared-control grants are not silently reacquired.

restore_without_rerun_downgrade.description: One session-scoped surface restored as transcript, evidence, reconnect-required, session-ended, rerun-required, or credential-gated instead of looking live again.

placeholder_card_id.description: Stable id for the placeholder card row. Survives across compare/export and re-render.

missing_dependency_ref.description: Opaque handle for the missing dependency (extension manifest entry, remote-target descriptor, permission ticket, service-dependency descriptor, schema-equivalence map). Null when the producer could not name a stable handle.

preserved_pane_id.description: The original stable pane id the placeholder card stands in for. A new pane id minted to substitute for a missing pane is non-conforming.

preserved_title_hint.description: Best-effort title hint preserved from the original pane. May be null.

evidence_retained.description: Whether a transcript, snapshot, or metadata-only summary of the original surface remained available behind the placeholder.

evidence_ref.description: Opaque ref to the retained evidence body, when one exists.

recovery_actions.description: Closed recovery actions surfaced on the placeholder card.

last_known_provenance_label.description: Short label describing the last-known live provenance of the surface (e.g. 'rust analyzer extension', 'remote python kernel'). Redaction-aware.

missing_dependency_placeholder_card.description: One missing-dependency placeholder card. Preserves the original pane role, evidence posture, and recovery actions instead of silently collapsing the surface out of the layout.

schema_migration_note.description: Schema-migration posture and the handles that explain how meaning travelled across versions.

intentional_exclusion_class.description: Closed taxonomy of intentional exclusions surfaced alongside missing dependencies. Intentional exclusions never imply a missing-dependency placeholder card.

intentional_exclusion_row.description: One intentionally-excluded class. Distinct from a missing dependency: the producer did not claim to carry this state in the first place.

preservation_reason.description: Why the prior artifact was preserved by reference.

rollback_note.description: Short retained rollback note describing why the prior artifact remains reachable. Redaction-aware.

preserved_prior_artifact_record.description: Ref and metadata for a prior artifact preserved before mutation.

$schema.description: Optional schema hint carried on worked fixtures.

restore_provenance_id.description: Stable id for the restore-provenance record.

source_schema_version.description: Source schema-version string from the producer artifact.

missing_dependency_classes.description: Aggregate inventory of missing-dependency classes detected by the restore. Empty when nothing was missing. Every class listed here MUST be the class of at least one row in missing_dependency_placeholder_cards.

missing_dependency_placeholder_cards.description: One placeholder-card row per pane that reopened only as a placeholder/context because of a missing dependency. Empty when no placeholder cards were emitted.

preserved_prior_artifacts.description: Prior artifacts retained for compare/export/rollback before mutation.

intentional_exclusions.description: Closed-class rows describing what the source intentionally never claimed to carry. Distinct from missing dependencies.

rollback_checkpoint_ref.description: Rollback checkpoint handle. Required for compatible, recovered-drafts, and evidence-only restores; null on exact restore.

equivalence_map_ref.description: Equivalence-map handle that explains the compatibility translation, if any. Required when schema_migration_note.note_class is schema_translation_applied or schema_meaning_changed.

compare_ref.description: Top-level compare handle for the resulting restore. Required on every fidelity above exact.

export_ref.description: Top-level export handle for the resulting restore. Required on every fidelity above exact.

restore_without_rerun_downgrades.description: Rows that explain surfaces restored without command rerun, live session reattachment, or hidden authority reacquire.

restore_provenance_record.description: Restore-provenance and missing-dependency placeholder record. Carries source, created-at, producer build, source schema version, redaction class, resulting fidelity, restore level, missing-dependency classes and placeholder cards, schema-migration notes, preserved-prior-artifact refs, intentional exclusions, and compare/export hooks so partial restores stay explicit instead of silent.
